message:
🌱 kubeflow: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code (#1862)

* 🌱 Add kubeflow: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code mission

* 🌱 Add kubeflow: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix kubeflow #3362 mission: remove wrong PR, fix resolution

Remove unrelated PR kubeflow/kubeflow#5570 (R-Studio images) from
solution sourceUrls. Fix resolution text to describe VS Code/JupyterLab
notebook support instead of R-Studio.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubeflow-3362-support-theia-or-microsoft-visual-studio-code",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubeflow: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code",
+    "description": "Support theia or microsoft visual studio code. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubeflow deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nhelm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubeflow installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubeflow configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nkubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow\n```\nIt is often useful to have a proper IDE when developing models. For example, even when using Jupyter, users may choose to organize python code into modules. They might also want to create additional files like YAML files."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support theia or microsoft visual studio code",
+        "description": "### Steps to build code-server on kubeflow:\n\n1. Install `code-server`: `curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh`\n1. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` : `pip3 install jupyter-server-proxy`\n1. Install `jupyter-vscode-proxy`: `pip3 install jupyter-vscode-proxy`\n1. Install `server-proxy` extension: `jupyter labextension install /server-proxy` or use extensions manager on jupyterlab frontend.\n\n### Steps to build other apps on kubeflow\n\n1. Install your app\n2. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` :\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nkubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support theia or microsoft visual studio code\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "### Steps to build code-server on kubeflow:\n\n1. Install `code-server`: `curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh`\n1. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` : `pip3 install jupyter-server-proxy`\n1. Install `jupyter-vscode-proxy`: `pip3 install jupyter-vscode-proxy`\n1. Install `server-proxy` extension: `jupyter labextension install /server-proxy` or use extensions manager on jupyterlab frontend.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },

+      "issue": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/3362",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow"
+    },
+    "reactions": 7,
+    "comments": 21,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",

+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible."

For example, even when using Jupyter, users may choose to organize python code into modules. They might also want to create additional files like YAML files."

+        "description": "Kubeflow eventually added support for multiple notebook IDE types beyond Jupyter, including VS Code (code-server) and JupyterLab. Users can now select their preferred IDE when creating a notebook server, enabling a full IDE experience for model development and file management within the Kubeflow environment.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."

+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nkubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support theia or microsoft visual studio code\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Kubeflow eventually added support for multiple notebook IDE types beyond Jupyter, including VS Code (code-server) and JupyterLab. Users can now select their preferred IDE when creating a notebook server, enabling a full IDE experience for model development and file management within the Kubeflow environment.",
